Book Consultation

Privacy Policy

Last updated: January 2025

1. Data Controller

[Consultant Name] ("we", "us", or "our") operates this website. For data protection inquiries, contact us at: contact@example.com.

2. Data We Collect

We collect the following categories of data:

  • Contact data: Name and email address submitted via our lead capture form.
  • Booking data: Name, email, and scheduling information submitted via Cal.com.
  • Usage data: Pages visited, time on site, and referral sources (via analytics cookies, only if consented).

3. Legal Basis for Processing

We process your data on the basis of your consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) when you submit the lead capture form or accept cookies, and on the basis of a pre-contractual relationship (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) when you book a consultation.

4. How We Use Your Data

  • To send you the requested downloadable resource.
  • To schedule and confirm consultations.
  • To send periodic insights and updates (only with explicit consent).
  • To improve our website performance and user experience.

5. Data Retention

We retain personal data for no longer than necessary for the purposes described above, or as required by law. Contact data is deleted within 24 months of last interaction unless a contractual relationship exists.

6. Your Rights

Under GDPR, you have the right to:

  • Access, correct, or delete your personal data.
  • Withdraw consent at any time.
  • Object to or restrict processing.
  • Lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority.

To exercise any of these rights, contact: contact@example.com

7. Cookies

We use strictly necessary cookies for site functionality and, with your consent, analytics cookies to understand how visitors interact with this website. You may withdraw consent at any time by clearing your browser cookies.

8. Third-Party Services

We use Cal.com for scheduling (subject to their Privacy Policy). We may use an email service provider for newsletter distribution. No data is sold to third parties.
